1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حم
1.english: Ha, Meem.
1.transliteration: Haa Meeem

1.arabic: والكتاب المبين
1.english: By the clear Book,
1.transliteration: Wal Kitaabil Mubeen

1.arabic: إنا أنزلناه في ليلة مباركة ۚ إنا كنا منذرين
1.english: Indeed, We sent it down during a blessed night. Indeed, We were to warn [mankind].
1.transliteration: Innaaa anzalnaahu fee lailatim mubaarakah; innaa kunnaa munzireen

1.arabic: فيها يفرق كل أمر حكيم
1.english: On that night is made distinct every precise matter -
1.transliteration: Feehaa yufraqu kullu amrin hakeem

1.arabic: أمرا من عندنا ۚ إنا كنا مرسلين
1.english: [Every] matter [proceeding] from Us. Indeed, We were to send [a messenger]
1.transliteration: Amram min 'indinaaa; innaa kunnaa mursileen

1.arabic: رحمة من ربك ۚ إنه هو السميع العليم
1.english: As mercy from your Lord. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Rahmatam mir rabbik; innahoo Huwas Samee'ul 'Aleem

1.arabic: رب السماوات والأرض وما بينهما ۖ إن كنتم موقنين
1.english: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that between them, if you would be certain.
1.transliteration: Rabbis samaawaati wal ardi wa maa bainahumaa; in kuntum mooqineen

1.arabic: لا إله إلا هو يحيي ويميت ۖ ربكم ورب آبائكم الأولين
1.english: There is no deity except Him; He gives life and causes death. [He is] your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers.
1.transliteration: Laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa yuhyee wa yumeetu Rabbukum wa Rabbu aabaaa'ikumul awwaleen

1.arabic: بل هم في شك يلعبون
1.english: But they are in doubt, amusing themselves.
1.transliteration: Bal hum fee shakkiny yal'aboon

1.arabic: فارتقب يوم تأتي السماء بدخان مبين
1.english: Then watch for the Day when the sky will bring a visible smoke.
1.transliteration: Fartaqib Yawma taatis samaaa'u bidukhaanim mubeen

1.arabic: يغشى الناس ۖ هذا عذاب أليم
1.english: Covering the people; this is a painful torment.
1.transliteration: Yaghshan naasa haazaa 'azaabun aleem

1.arabic: ربنا اكشف عنا العذاب إنا مؤمنون
1.english: [They will say], "Our Lord, remove from us the torment; indeed, we are believers."
1.transliteration: Rabbanak shif 'annal 'azaaba innaa mu'minoon

1.arabic: أنى لهم الذكرى وقد جاءهم رسول مبين
1.english: How will there be for them a reminder [at that time]? And there had come to them a clear Messenger.
1.transliteration: Annaa lahumuz zikraa wa qad jaaa'ahum Rasoolum mubeen

1.arabic: ثم تولوا عنه وقالوا معلم مجنون
1.english: Then they turned away from him and said, "[He was] taught [and is] a madman."
1.transliteration: Summaa tawallaw 'anhu wa qaaloo mu'allamum majnoon

1.arabic: إنا كاشفو العذاب قليلا ۚ إنكم عائدون
1.english: Indeed, We will remove the torment for a little. Indeed, you [disbelievers] will return [to disbelief].
1.transliteration: Innaa kaashiful 'azaabi qaleelaa; innakum 'aaa'indoon

1.arabic: يوم نبطش البطشة الكبرى إنا منتقمون
1.english: The Day We will strike with the greatest assault, indeed, We will take retribution.
1.transliteration: Yawma nabtishul batsha tal kubraa innaa muntaqimoon

1.arabic: ۞ ولقد فتنا قبلهم قوم فرعون وجاءهم رسول كريم
1.english: And We had already tried before them the people of Pharaoh, and there came to them a noble messenger,
1.transliteration: Wa laqad fatannaa qablahum qawma Fir'awna wa jaaa'ahum Rasoolun kareem

1.arabic: أن أدوا إلي عباد الله ۖ إني لكم رسول أمين
1.english: [Saying], "Render to me the servants of Allah. Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger,"
1.transliteration: An addooo ilaiya 'ibaadal laahi innee lakum Rasoolun ameen

1.arabic: وأن لا تعلوا على الله ۖ إني آتيكم بسلطان مبين
1.english: And [saying], "Be not haughty with Allah. Indeed, I have come to you with clear authority.
1.transliteration: Wa al laa ta'loo 'alal laahi innee aateekum bisultaanim mubeen

1.arabic: وإني عذت بربي وربكم أن ترجمون
1.english: And indeed, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord, lest you stone me.
1.transliteration: Wa innee 'uztu bi Rabbee wa rabbikum an tarjumoon

1.arabic: وإن لم تؤمنوا لي فاعتزلون
1.english: But if you do not believe me, then leave me alone."
1.transliteration: Wa il lam tu'minoo lee fa'taziloon

1.arabic: فدعا ربه أن هؤلاء قوم مجرمون
1.english: And [finally] he called to his Lord that these were a criminal people.
1.transliteration: Fada'aa rabbahooo anna haaa'ulaaa'i qawmum mujrimoon

1.arabic: فأسر بعبادي ليلا إنكم متبعون
1.english: [Allah said], "Then set out with My servants by night. Indeed, you are to be pursued.
1.transliteration: Fa asri bi'ibaadee lailan innakum muttaba'oon

1.arabic: واترك البحر رهوا ۖ إنهم جند مغرقون
1.english: And leave the sea in stillness. Indeed, they are an army to be drowned."
1.transliteration: Watrukil bahra rahwan innahum jundum mughraqoon

1.arabic: كم تركوا من جنات وعيون
1.english: How much they left behind of gardens and springs
1.transliteration: Kam tarakoo min jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.arabic: وزروع ومقام كريم
1.english: And crops and noble sites
1.transliteration: Wa zuroo'inw wa maqaa min kareem

1.arabic: ونعمة كانوا فيها فاكهين
1.english: And comfort wherein they were amused.
1.transliteration: Wa na'matin kaanoo feehaa faakiheen

1.arabic: كذلك ۖ وأورثناها قوما آخرين
1.english: Thus. And We caused to inherit it another people.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika wa awrasnaahaa qawman aakhareen

1.arabic: فما بكت عليهم السماء والأرض وما كانوا منظرين
1.english: And the heaven and earth wept not for them, nor were they reprieved.
1.transliteration: Famaa bakat 'alaihimus samaaa'u wal ardu wa maa kaanoo munzareen

1.arabic: ولقد نجينا بني إسرائيل من العذاب المهين
1.english: And We certainly saved the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment -
1.transliteration: Wa laqad najjainaa Baneee Israaa'eela minal'azaabil muheen

1.arabic: من فرعون ۚ إنه كان عاليا من المسرفين
1.english: From Pharaoh. Indeed, he was a haughty one among the transgressors.
1.transliteration: Min Fir'awn; innahoo kaana 'aaliyam minal musrifeen

1.arabic: ولقد اخترناهم على علم على العالمين
1.english: And We certainly chose them by knowledge over [all] the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa laqadikh tarnaahum 'alaa 'ilmin 'alal 'aalameen

1.arabic: وآتيناهم من الآيات ما فيه بلاء مبين
1.english: And We gave them of signs that in which there was a clear trial.
1.transliteration: Wa aatainaahum minal aayaati maa feehi balaaa'um mubeen

1.arabic: إن هؤلاء ليقولون
1.english: Indeed, these [disbelievers] are saying,
1.transliteration: Inna haaa'ulaaa'i la yaqooloon

1.arabic: إن هي إلا موتتنا الأولى وما نحن بمنشرين
1.english: "There is not but our first death, and we will not be resurrected.
1.transliteration: In hiya illaa mawtatunal oolaa wa maa nahnu bimun shareen

1.arabic: فأتوا بآبائنا إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: Then bring [back] our forefathers, if you should be truthful."
1.transliteration: Faatoo bi aabaaa'inaaa inkuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: أهم خير أم قوم تبع والذين من قبلهم ۚ أهلكناهم ۖ إنهم كانوا مجرمين
1.english: Are they better or the people of Tubba' and those before them? We destroyed them, [for] indeed, they were criminals.
1.transliteration: Ahum khayrun am qawmu Tubba'inw wallazeena min qablihim; ahlaknaahum innahum kaanoo mujrimeen

1.arabic: وما خلقنا السماوات والأرض وما بينهما لاعبين
1.english: And We did not create the heavens and earth and that between them in play.
1.transliteration: Wa maa khalaqnas samaawaati wal arda wa maa baina humaa laa'ibeen

1.arabic: ما خلقناهما إلا بالحق ولكن أكثرهم لا يعلمون
1.english: We did not create them except in truth, but most of them do not know.
1.transliteration: Maa khalaqnaahumaaa illaa bilhaqqi wa laakinna aksarahum laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: إن يوم الفصل ميقاتهم أجمعين
1.english: Indeed, the Day of Judgement is the appointed time for them all -
1.transliteration: Inna yawmal fasli meeqaatuhum ajma'een

1.arabic: يوم لا يغني مولى عن مولى شيئا ولا هم ينصرون
1.english: The Day when no relation will avail a relation at all, nor will they be helped -
1.transliteration: Yawma laa yughnee mawlan 'am mawlan shai'anw wa laa hum yunsaroon

1.arabic: إلا من رحم الله ۚ إنه هو العزيز الرحيم
1.english: Except those [believers] on whom Allah has mercy. Indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Illaa mar rahimal laah' innahoo huwal 'azeezur raheem

1.arabic: إن شجرت الزقوم
1.english: Indeed, the tree of zaqqum
1.transliteration: Inna shajarataz zaqqoom

1.arabic: طعام الأثيم
1.english: Is food for the sinful.
1.transliteration: Ta'aamul aseem

1.arabic: كالمهل يغلي في البطون
1.english: Like murky oil, it boils within bellies
1.transliteration: Kalmuhli yaghlee filbutoon

1.arabic: كغلي الحميم
1.english: Like the boiling of scalding water.
1.transliteration: Kaghalyil hameem

1.arabic: خذوه فاعتلوه إلى سواء الجحيم
1.english: [It will be commanded], "Seize him and drag him into the midst of the Hellfire,
1.transliteration: Khuzoohu fa'tiloohu ilaa sawaaa'il Jaheem

1.arabic: ثم صبوا فوق رأسه من عذاب الحميم
1.english: Then pour over his head from the torment of scalding water."
1.transliteration: Summa subboo fawqa raasihee min 'azaabil hameem

1.arabic: ذق إنك أنت العزيز الكريم
1.english: [It will be said], "Taste! Indeed, you are the honored, the noble!
1.transliteration: Zuq innaka antal 'azeezul kareem

1.arabic: إن هذا ما كنتم به تمترون
1.english: Indeed, this is what you used to dispute."
1.transliteration: Inna haazaa maa kuntum bihee tamtaroon

1.arabic: إن المتقين في مقام أمين
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place;
1.transliteration: Innal muttaqeena fee maqaamin ameen

1.arabic: في جنات وعيون
1.english: Within gardens and springs,
1.transliteration: Fee jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.arabic: يلبسون من سندس وإستبرق متقابلين
1.english: Wearing [garments of] fine silk and brocade, facing each other.
1.transliteration: Yalbasoona min sundusinw wa istbraqim mutaqaabileen

1.arabic: كذلك وزوجناهم بحور عين
1.english: Thus. And We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika wa zawwajnaahum bihoorin 'een

1.arabic: يدعون فيها بكل فاكهة آمنين
1.english: They will call therein for every [kind of] fruit - safe and secure.
1.transliteration: Yad'oona feehaa bikulli faakihatin aamineen

1.arabic: لا يذوقون فيها الموت إلا الموتة الأولى ۖ ووقاهم عذاب الجحيم
1.english: They will not taste death therein except the first death, and He will have protected them from the punishment of Hellfire
1.transliteration: Laa yazooqoona feehal mawtaa illal mawtatal oolaa wa qaqaahum 'azaabal jaheem

1.arabic: فضلا من ربك ۚ ذلك هو الفوز العظيم
1.english: As bounty from your Lord. That is what is the great attainment.
1.transliteration: Fadlam mir rabbik; zaalika huwal fawzul 'azeem

1.arabic: فإنما يسرناه بلسانك لعلهم يتذكرون
1.english: And indeed, We have eased the Qur'an in your tongue that they might be reminded.
1.transliteration: Fa innamaa yassarnaahu bilisaanika la'allahum yatazakkaroon

1.arabic: فارتقب إنهم مرتقبون
1.english: So watch, [O Muhammad]; indeed, they are watching [for your end].
1.transliteration: Fartaqib innahum murta qiboon
